name: Maple Pecan Crescent Twists
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Lightly grease a large baking sheet or two small ones.
3. In a small bowl, mix together chopped pecans, sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg until well combined.
4. Unroll the dough and separate into 8 rectangles.
5. Firmly press the perforations to seal each rectangle.
6. Brush each rectangle with melted margarine.
7. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of the sugar-nut mixture evenly over each rectangle, pressing in lightly.
8. Starting at the longer side, roll up each rectangle tightly and pinch edges to seal.
9. With a sharp knife, cut each roll into halves lengthwise, creating two strips.
10. Lay the strips with the cut side up, overlapping them twice to form a twist. Press the ends together to seal.
11. Place the twisted rolls on the prepared baking sheet.
12. Repeat with remaining dough.
13. Sprinkle any remaining sugar-nut mixture over the rolls.
14. Bake for 10-15 minutes or until golden brown.
15. In a small bowl, whisk powdered sugar, maple extract, and milk until smooth for drizzling glaze.
16. Brush glaze over warm rolls before serving.
Note: If refrigerating rolled dough before cutting, chill for 5 minutes to make it easier. Rolls can be prepared ahead of time, covered, and refrigerated overnight, then cut, twisted, and baked in the morning.
